rulings.Case Title: LISA ARNOLD VS. CLARA ANI BISHOP ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT / Notice Of Joinder And Joinder To Defendant Navajo Express, Inc.'S Motion For Summary Judgment Or, In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities In Support Thereof; Declaratio Of Gregory P. Gilmer
rulings.Rulings: On the Law and Motion / Discovery calendar for March 12, 2026, line 1. DEFENDANT FCA, US, LLC'S Joinder To Defendant Navajo Express, Inc.'s Motion For Summary Judgment Or, In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication. FCA US LLC's joinder in Navajo Express, Inc.'s Motion for Summary Judgment, or in the Alternative, Summary Adjudication, is granted. FCA US LLC's motion is premised entirely on its argument that plaintiff Lisa Arnold lacks statutory standing to bring this wrongful death action. For the reasons explained in the court's companion order granting Navajo Express, Inc.'s summary judgment motion, Arnold lacks statutory standing because she is the grandparent of the decedent, whose parents are living and had parental rights. The motion for summary judgment is therefore granted. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(301/CVA)

rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Stay, Or In The Alternative, For A Protective Order That The Discovery Directed To Defendants Jia Lin Zheng, Youqiao Liu And Allen Guowai Zheng Not To Proceed
rulings.Rulings: On the Law and Motion / Discovery calendar for March 12, 2026, line 7. DEFENDANTS ALLEN ZHENG, YOUQIAO LIU, JIALIN ZHENG'S Motion To Stay, Or In The Alternative, For A Protective Order That The Discovery Directed To Defendants Jia Lin Zheng, Youqiao Liu And Allen Guowai Zheng Not To Proceed. The unopposed motion for stay is granted. The case is stayed in its entirety for all purposes until the conclusion of the criminal case against Jialin Zheng. The stay extends to served discovery, which need not be answered and may be re-served when the stay is lifted. rulings.Case Title: GARY M WAITE VS. EDWARD M HIGGINBOTHAM
rulings.Rulings: On the Law and Motion / Discovery calendar for March 12, 2026, line 8. DEFENDANT BRYANT LAW GROUP LLP'S DEMURRER to 2ND Amended COMPLAINT. Defendant BRYANT LAW GROUP LLP's demurrer to the second amended complaint is sustained with 20 days leave to amend. The court exercises its discretion and hears this untimely demurrer. (CCP 430.40(a) [party "may" file a demurrer within 30 days of service]; McAllister v. County of Monterey (2007) 147 Cal.App.4th 253, 281-282 [noting trial court's discretion to hear untimely demurrer]. The court sustains the demurrer for uncertainty. (CCP 430.10(f).) Plaintiff has leave to amend to plead causes of action for attorney malpractice, breach of contract, breach of fiduciary duty, and fraud. The third amended complaint must allege "[a] statement of the facts... in ordinary and concise language." (CCP section 425.10(a)(1).) Plaintiff currently alleges repetitive and incomplete claims. For example, plaintiff alleges causes of action for "Duty of Care," "Causation," and "Damages." Those are the elements of a cause of action for negligence, not independent causes of action. (See Thomas v. Stenberg (2012) 206 Cal.App.4th 654, 662.) Plaintiff shall remove the redundancies and unnecessary caselaw citations. The third amended complaint shall comply with CRC 2.112 and each cause of action shall identify against whom it is alleged. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(301/CVA)

rulings.Case Title: BRENDA MARZOL ET AL VS. MCLANE SUNEAST D/B/A MCLANE PACIFIC ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Vacate Order Dismissing Second Amended Complaint With Prejudice; Request To Proceed On Third Amended Complaint
rulings.Rulings: On the Law and Motion / Discovery calendar for March 12, 2026, line 10. PLAINTIFFS BRENDA MARZOL, AARON VOLPONI'S Motion To Vacate Order Dismissing Second Amended Complaint With Prejudice; Request To Proceed On Third Amended Complaint. The unopposed motion to vacate order dismissing second amended complaint is continued for hearing to April 28, 2026. The motion does not contain a proof of service. Plaintiffs are ordered to serve their motion on all parties who have appeared in this case and to file a proof of service by no later than March 19, 2026. Any opposition may be filed by nine court days in advance of the continued hearing date. Any reply may be filed by five court days in advance of the continued hearing date. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom.
